folio.label: 72r
folio.l: For thougú the Pope , had siten hem beside
I wold nat spare hem , at here owen bord
For be my trouthe , I quyt hem word for word
As help me verray god , omnipotent
Thougú I rigút now , shulde make my testament´
I owe hem nat a word , that it nys quytte
I brought it so a boute , be my wytte
That they must ©eue it vp , for the best
Or elles had we neuäe , ben in rest
ffor thougú he loked , as a wod lyouö
ñet shulde he faile , of his conclusiouö
Than wold I sey , good lef take kepe
How mekely loketú , wylkyn oure shepe
Come neer my spouse , lat me ba thy cheke
ñe shulden be , al pacient and meke
And han a swete , spiced conscience
Sithe ©e so preche , of Iobes pacience
Suffretú al wey , syn ©e so wel can preche
And but ©e do , certeyn we shuln ©ow teche
That it is fair , to han a wyf in pees
Oon of vs two , must bowen doutelees
And sithe a man , is more resonable
Than womman is , ©e must ben suffrable
What eyletú ©ow , to grucche thus and grone
It is for ©e wolde , han my queynte allone
We take it al , lo haue it euäydele
Petir I shrewe ©ow , but ©e loue it wele
For if I wolde selle , my beal Chose
I coude walke , as fressú as any Rose
But I wol kepe it , for ©oure owen totú
ñe be to blame , by god I seye ©ow sotú
Swiche maner wordes , had we on honde
Now wol I speke , of my first husbonde
¢ My first husbonde , was a reuelour
This is to seyn , he had a paramour
And I was ©ongö , and ful of ragerie
Stiburne and strongö , and ioly as a Pye
Tho coude I daunce , to an harp smale
And synge I wysse , as any nytynggale
Whan I had dronke , a draugút of swete wyn
Metellius the foul cherl , the swyn
That witú a staf , by reft his wyf hishirhisr lif
For she drank wyn , thougú I had ben his wyf
He shulde nat han daunted , me fro drynke
And after wyn , on venus must I thynke
For also siker , as cold , engenderetú hail
